sourceMovie.title: Big Daddy
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Big Daddy (1999) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Dennis Dugan's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Carnage
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Big Daddy clicked for you, Carnage (2011) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Big Daddy?
faq.answer: Try Carnage, The Spectacular Now, Fatherhood—each shares DNA with Big Daddy while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Big Daddy?
faq.answer: Big Daddy (1999) is best described as Comedy, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Big Daddy worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
